title: mauranthemum reboudianum
intro: Mauranthemum reboudianum, also known as the Rebour's daisy, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its white petals and yellow centers. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Mauranthemum reboudianum is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues, including skin infections and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide several health benefits, including soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Mauranthemum reboudianum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams, offering a natural and potentially effective treatment option for various health concerns.
